text: Eight weeks of continuous moderate-intensity training reduces energy cost of running by approximately 4.4% in master runners.
Eight weeks of discontinuous high-intensity training reduces energy cost of running by approximately 4.9% in master runners.
Both continuous moderate-intensity and discontinuous high-intensity training improve 5-km time-trial performance in master runners by 2-3% over 8 weeks.
Discontinuous high-intensity training increases peak running speed by 6% in master runners, while continuous moderate-intensity training does not.
Master runners can improve running economy and 5-km performance with as little as 35 km per week of structured training, even when reducing from higher weekly volumes (~50 km/week).
Neither continuous moderate-intensity nor discontinuous high-intensity training significantly improved VO2peak in master runners over 8 weeks.
Both moderate-intensity and high-intensity training increase speed at gas exchange threshold in master runners, with high-intensity showing a greater improvement (5.7% vs 3.4%).
